reasoning: The Lean statement correctly captures the informal claim. H being "the unique subgroup of a given order" is encoded as: H has a Fintype instance (finite order) and for every finite subgroup K with the same cardinality, K = H. The quantification over Fintype instances on K is harmless since Fintype is essentially unique up to cardinality; and any subgroup with the same cardinality as the finite H is automatically finite. The conclusion `H.Characteristic` matches "H is characteristic in G", and G is properly universally quantified. The Fintype H assumption aligns with the standard interpretation of "a given order" as a specified finite cardinality in this classical Dummit & Foote exercise.
